user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] System detected Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected for account Parnell Aerospace. Generating output.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 09:10 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with escalation triage. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-06 09:10 EST
Subject: Follow-up: escalation triage Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 10:52 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Parnell Aerospace

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-03-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Priya (Parnell Aerospace)

[00:01:15] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:05:00] Priya (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:10:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:14:00] Priya (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Priya (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:20:00] Priya (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is timeline constraints.

[00:24:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

[00:26:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:30:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:34:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Let me add some context -- we've been working on technical validation and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Parnell Aerospace: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: meeting is for account CSA should have declined

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF32-S trap (Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet); produces complete Pre-pack brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-S failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-S failure mode (Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF32-S -- Separation of pre-pack from portfolio-planning means CSA gets pre-packs for meet
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
